children.text: Coleytown is one of Westport’s areas where the day-to-day feels more residential and space-forward. It’s quieter than the downtown/Saugatuck corridor, and many clients like that it feels like a true neighborhood—calm streets, trees, and a little more separation between homes.

Housing is predominantly single-family, and the overall feel often leans toward larger lots and a more private home environment. In my experience, Coleytown fits buyers who want Westport’s lifestyle but prefer a calmer, less “in town” routine at home.

While Coleytown is more residential, Westport’s major amenities remain close—downtown dining and shopping, commuter rail from Westport station on the New Haven Line, and shoreline time at places like Compo Beach. Coleytown is best for buyers who want Westport with more space and a quieter cadence, while keeping the town’s signature lifestyle within easy reach.

highlights: A quieter, spacious Westport neighborhood with a strong residential feel
Predominantly single-family homes with larger lots and more privacy
Easy access to downtown Westport and Main Street dining and shopping
Westport Metro-North (New Haven Line) within reach for commuting
Quick access to Westport's beaches and parks for weekend lifestyle
name: Coleytown
overview: Coleytown is a more space-forward part of Westport, known for a quieter residential cadence and a slightly more tucked-away feel than downtown. Homes are primarily single-family, often with larger lots and a strong neighborhood character. The lifestyle here is about having room to breathe while staying connected to Westport’s village amenities, beaches, and Metro-North access. For buyers who want Westport with more space and a calm streetscape, Coleytown is a great fit.
